content: Let's continue working on the nanobot memory system. I want to check in on the extraction pipeline we've been building.
Sure! The extraction pipeline is coming along well. We have the Haiku-based extraction working with the XML-tagged transcript format. What aspect do you want to focus on?
I want to make sure the dedup logic is solid. We're using LanceDB with text-embedding-3-small at 512 dimensions, right?
Yes, exactly. LanceDB with openai/text-embedding-3-small at 512 dimensions. The dedup thresholds we settled on are 0.95 for identical (reinforce only) and 0.85 for related (needs review).
Right. And the extraction runs every 20 user turns, plus on /new and before compaction as a catch-up step.
Correct. The trigger conditions are: 20 completed user turns since last extraction, compaction catch-up (messages about to leave window), session end via /new, and idle flush after 30 minutes.
One thing I want to revisit — the extraction model. We're using Haiku 3.5, but I'm wondering if Haiku 4.5 would be better now.

Haiku 4.5 would be a meaningful upgrade — better instruction following and less likely to hallucinate. Since extraction is a background operation and cost is low per run, the upgrade makes sense. Should we switch?
Yes, let's use Haiku 4.5 going forward for extraction. The background_model config should be updated to claude-haiku-4-5.
Got it. I'll update the background_model config to claude-haiku-4-5 for the extraction pipeline.
Also, I confirmed yesterday that the watermark only advances after successful write to the graph. That's the critical rule we need to enforce.

Yes, that's fundamental. The watermark (`last_extracted_index`) must only advance after ALL items from the batch are successfully committed to the graph. Any failure keeps the watermark in place for retry at the next trigger.
Good. And the memory graph is on LanceDB — that part isn't changing.
Correct, LanceDB stays. It handles the vector + graph edge storage well.
